HO-Azure :: attack-path-focused Azure recon
context :: tenant=auto subscription=auto output=table

[persistence] Azure Functions persistence means a hosted Function App plus deployed code plus trigger posture plus access that Azure can invoke later, not a long-running process on a VM.
Azure Functions capability

╭──────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┬────────╮
│ action                                   │ api surface                             │ status │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
│ create or modify function app            │ Microsoft.Web/sites                     │ yes    │
│ deploy or replace code                   │ zip deploy / run-from-package / publish │ yes    │
│ repurpose trigger posture                │ HTTP, timer, queue, or event trigger    │ yes    │
│ change app settings or deployment config │ app settings / site config              │ yes    │
│ attach or reuse exec ctx                 │ function identity / app settings        │ yes    │
│ restart or enable function host          │ site state / restart action             │ yes    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────┴────────╯
- Current identity can create a new Function App or reuse this existing Function App.
   The Function App is the Azure-side host for function code, app settings, identity, trigger configuration, and the deployed package.

- Current identity can deploy or replace the function package Azure will load in this Function App.
   Because the current identity already controls this Function App, zip deploy, publish, or package replacement are part of the defended Functions persistence path here.
   Common deploy paths here include ZIP package deployment, pipeline deployment, run-from-package, or local project publish.
   Visible deployment posture includes storage=plain-text.
   Visible deployment posture includes kv-refs=1.
   AzureWebJobsStorage is plain-text.

- Current identity can repurpose this Function App's trigger exposure so Azure has a way to run it again later, including HTTP-style externally reachable entrypoints.
   Visible child functions here show `HTTP`, `timer`, and `Service Bus` trigger paths.
   Current visible functions include OrdersWebhook [HTTP], NightlyReconcile [timer], BusReconcile [Service Bus; disabled].
   HTTP-triggered functions are visible from management-plane metadata, including an invoke URL template and visible auth-level metadata.
   Timer, queue, Service Bus, or other event-driven triggers are visible from bindings, but they are not the same as a directly callable public entrypoint.
   The remaining gap is data-plane and runtime-side validation the current management-plane collector does not perform.
   That includes function keys or caller auth actually in hand, upstream Service Bus or storage access, and any runtime-side restriction beyond the visible trigger metadata.

- Current identity can change app settings, identity attachment, and deployment configuration for this Function App.
   This is where runtime behavior, package behavior, and connection material get shaped for this Function App.
   App settings can control service endpoints, feature toggles, and other runtime behavior the function will use when the trigger fires.

- Current identity can attach or reuse execution context for this Function App.
   In Functions, execution context usually comes from the managed identity attached to this Function App.
   That can be a system-assigned identity, a user-assigned identity, or connection material referenced through settings the code will use later.
   Current foothold `azurefox-lab-sp` already holds Owner at subscription scope.
   The strongest visible execution context here is the attached user-assigned identity `ua-orders`, which already holds Owner at subscription scope.

- Current identity can restart or enable the Function App so Azure is ready to run it again when the trigger path is hit.
   Once the package is in place and the Function App is active, Azure can invoke it whenever the trigger condition is met.

- Current identity can repurpose an existing Function App instead of creating a new one.
   Reusing an existing Function App can blend in better than standing up a brand-new serverless entrypoint.

Visible Function Apps
function app | resource group | visible state                                                                                                                          | execution context                           

func-orders  | rg-apps        | Running; hostname visible; PYTHON|3.11; functions=~4; storage=plain-text; kv-refs=1; public Enabled; triggers=HTTP, timer, Service Bus | `ua-orders` with Owner at subscription scope


Not collected by default
- the current default collector does not perform data-plane validation of function keys, caller auth, upstream bus or storage access, or other runtime-side restrictions behind the visible management-plane trigger metadata
- the current command does not retrieve deployed function packages or project contents, so operator intent is not inferred from code here
- the current command does not infer downstream resource actions, secret usage, or service connections without reading deployed code or exercising the function path
